tax: Ignis Elementalis Major is placed at species rank beneath Ignis (Pyric order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a 4–6 m deep red-orange flame humanoid with a 1.5 m char-circle footprint, formed at vent convergences. It is separated from its sibling Ignis elementalis by its 4–6 m deep red-orange body from a vent convergence versus the standard form's 2–3 m orange-yellow body from a single vent; the two share the genus body-plan but never the single-vent formation. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: deep red-orange combustion in a massive humanoid shape sustained by convergent multi-vent fuel.
ap: Ignis Elementalis Major stands 4–6 m and the thermal output of multiple forges. Its silhouette is a massive humanoid pillar of deep red-orange flame with a near-white inner core. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: deep red-orange colour — lower temperature than the minor but far greater volume — producing a 10 m radiant-heat zone that kills exposed skin in under a minute; a char-circle 1.5 m across at each footstep position, blackened 8 cm deep; and ceiling blackening visible 20 m along its passage even after it has moved on, indicating it has held the space for extended periods. It moves with a slow powerful float that chars the ceiling as well as the floor, leaving a 20 m black passage where it has walked, and shows deep red-orange with near-white core, visible ambient glow at 200 m; radiant heat felt at 20 m. Even after dispersal it can be identified from 1.5 m char-circles, ceiling blackening to 20 m, and a near-total absence of flammable material along its passage, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Ignis Elementalis Major persists in major vent convergences and lava-floor galleries of the lower tiers where multiple fuel streams sustain a massive combustion mass. It does not breed; it forms where convergent multi-vent fuel pressure ignites a massive combustion form into a sustained humanoid shape, and recurs whenever the vent convergence refuels and the massive form re-ignites from the same convergence zone. It sustains its mass on convergent multi-vent fuel from two or more vent-faults, drawing in multi-stream vent fuel drawn from the convergence to sustain the massive volume. Range is bounded by the multi-vent convergence zone: outside that band the body reduces to standard size where the secondary vents fail, then disperses. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a passage where two vent-fuelled zones have begun producing a shared bilateral gas-flame at their junction, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Ignis Elementalis Major is roused by any crew entering its passage or any reduction in its fuel supply (which makes it range-extend to find more) and otherwise lies a pillar of slow deep fire filling a passage, potentially mistaken for a vent field or lava glow. It claims a single vent convergence and the passages that feed it and answers intrusion with filling the passage with its body width to block retreat, then advancing; it does not dart or corral — it simply fills the space. Toward grey-folk it is an extreme continuous-radiant-heat hazard; survey crews report that Forgemasters do not approach a passage with ceiling-blackening over 15 m of continuous span; that span indicates the major form has held it. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Ignis Elementalis Major: a body of deep red-orange combustion in a massive humanoid shape sustained by convergent multi-vent fuel; immune to fire, heat, and physical impact; the vent convergence resupplies it through partial smothering faster than the standard form; and a defining weakness — total convergent fuel cut-off requiring all supply vents to be sealed simultaneously — sealing one vent only redirects and enrages it. Its senses run through the medium itself: radiant-heat sensing at 300 m through the vent convergence; it detects warm bodies at the edge of its passage before they see the red glow. It tolerates extreme heat, fuel-rich air, and enclosed passages but fails at simultaneous sealing of all supply vents. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the 4–6 m deep red-orange body, 1.5 m char-circle, and ceiling-blackened passage, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Ignis Elementalis Major are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it fills the passage width with its body, blocking all retreat routes; it advances to maintain contact with its convergence fuel supply, stretching up to 20 m from the junction while sustaining full form; it re-fuels through partial smothering if even one supply vent remains open; and it radiates killing heat at 10 m without direct contact — a crew can be fatally burned without ever touching the body. Each is a direct expression of convergent multi-vent fuel in a massive deep-red humanoid combustion form, so it cannot, for instance, fit into passages under 2 m wide, move quickly, or sustain itself when all supply vents are sealed. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Ignis Elementalis Major matters because ceiling-blackening over 15 m of continuous passage span is the definitive field sign of a major form territory, and Forgemasters now refuse all drilling leases within 200 m of a 15 m blackened span. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as an extreme vent-convergence hazard requiring simultaneous multi-vent sealing and mandatory black-span survey before any lease, and its presence reshapes routes through the lower vent-convergence passages, now hard-banned within 200 m of a continuous 15 m ceiling-blackened span. The Forgemasters' saying 'the ceiling tells you where it lives' references the major form's habit of holding a passage for days, blackening the stone above, before ranging out.
g: Ignis Elementalis Major (Ignis elementalis major) is the great-form fire elemental: a 4–6 m towering Ignis sustained by a major vent convergence, capable of filling a passage with flame and sustaining it for hours. Documented from the major vent convergences and fuel-rich lava-floor galleries of the lower tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a massive humanoid pillar of deep red-orange flame with a near-white core that radiates killing heat at 10 m — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The major form is not a sustained standard form — it forms only where two or more vent-faults converge, and its deeper red-orange colour reflects a richer but slower burn than standard. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a wide char-circle 1.5 m across and blackened walls up to the ceiling along its entire passage length and no carcass.
